name: Sarah McBride
subject: A quick note to say thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
2025 felt like a hell of a decade.
#
Happy New Year!
Before anything else, I need to say this: _thank you._
2025 felt like a hell of a decade. Between Donald Trump and Republican politicians’ chaos and cruelty, and the real financial stress families are under, it wasn’t an easy year.
In moments like that, it’s easy to feel worn down, and yet you continued to show up.
As we closed out the year, I saw people step up to support our campaign in every possible way. Some chipped in $1. Some gave what they could afford. Some wanted to give but couldn’t — and instead sent notes of encouragement and stayed engaged. Every single one of those actions mattered.
Our campaign isn’t powered by right-wing billionaire megadonors or corporate special interests. It’s powered by people like you who believe that government should work for working families.
We’re still finalizing our end-of-year numbers, but I already know that because of you, we’re starting 2026 with strength. And that’s important because the weeks and months ahead are critical.
We have a real shot at taking back the House, refocusing Congress on lowering costs and holding Donald Trump accountable, and delivering results for Delawareans and folks across the country. It’s going to take all of us — and I’m so glad you’re in this with me and all of us at Team McBride.
So thank you again.
With deep gratitude,
Sarah
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20260102-1&t=1&refcode2=2636_21716_eTGlsG&akid=2636%2E21716%2EeTGlsG)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=3&akid=2636%2E21716%2EeTGlsG)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2636%2E21716%2EeTGlsG)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

name: Research Survey (Voter Action Project)
subject: Options for voter ID at the polls
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
@RecorderDannyO: **Important** It is with great disappointment that I share that the Veteran Photo-ID Cards issued by Ohio County Recorders’ Offices appear to no longer be considered eligible forms of voter ID. This change is part of the new voter ID law, HB 458, signed into law on 1/6/23. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/Ohio_VeteransID.png]
Ohio counties are grappling with the impact of their voter suppression law that enacts stringent voter identification laws.
Franklin County, home to the state capitol of Columbus and more than a million Ohioans, is among the counties that are no longer able to accept veterans photo ID cards issued by the county as proof for voting because of Ohio's law.
Team: We're looking to hear from our supporters about this issue. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Should states with voter ID requirements expand or further restrict the types of IDs they accept?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
EXPAND [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=010226_2expand&amount=15] RESTRICT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=010226_2restrict&amount=15]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=010226_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=010226_2&amount=20]

We can beat back this wave of dark money, but it starts with what we raise right now. Will you chip in $20 or more to help us fight back and flip the House in 2026? name: Tom Fitton
subject: Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton Receives Presidential Reappointment
body:
Suing for Human Fetal Tissue Research Records

[INSIDE JW]

PRESIDENT TRUMP REAPPOINTS TOM FITTON TO DC JUDICIAL COMMISSION

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36787&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9nTjVJOHBxVnNRbz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=e805fd874821a4929601e00ee9f5c3da5b8505acf6e75b28e6dc000720e6ef7f]

I am pleased to announce that President Trump has reappointed me to
the District of Columbia Commission on Judicial Disabilities and
Tenure
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36760&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9jamR0LmRjLmdvdi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=63c90e27db503d9281d957cacbde020c478bc583c226a0124b6f874ae374e93d]
for a five-year term. He initially
appointed me to the commission in 2020. I issued this statement about
the reappointment:

> Thank you, President Trump, for reappointing me to the District of
> Columbia Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure. I am
> honored and humbled by your confidence. This important
> responsibility is of course in addition to my duties as Judicial
> Watch president. I look forward to my continued work with my fellow
> commissioners to ‘maintain public confidence in an independent,
> impartial, fair, and qualified judiciary, and to enforce the high
> standards of conduct judges must adhere to both on and off the
> bench.’ Our nation’s capital needs the best possible courts,
> especially in light of the public safety concerns that have gained
> national attention.

The District of Columbia Commission on Judicial Disabilities and
Tenure was established by Congress to review complaints of misconduct
against judges of the District of Columbia courts. The commission’s
authority includes reappointment evaluations (sent to the president)
of associate judges and performance and fitness reviews of senior
judges.

The commission
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=20726&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9jamR0LmRjLmdvdi9wYWdlL2Fib3V0LWNqZHQ/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=d5cd613325e08535e1603361e815ed635b61d08357aa457d19df9b9defa83f5a]
has “the
authority to remove a judge for willful misconduct in office, for
willful and persistent failure to perform judicial duties, and for
conduct prejudicial to the administration of justice, or which brings
the judicial office into disrepute. The Commission also has the
authority to retire a judge involuntarily if the Commission determines
that the judge suffers from a mental or physical disability which is
or is likely to become permanent and which prevents, or seriously
interferes with, the proper performance of duties. In addition, the
Commission may, under appropriate circumstances, censure or reprimand
a judge publicly.”

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES HHS FOR HUMAN FETAL TISSUE RESEARCH RECORDS

Americans have a right to know basic information about the
taxpayer-funded abortion industrial complex. As long as the government
continues to fund these gruesome projects, we will work to expose
them.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36776&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtaGhzLXUtcGl0dC1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDQ0OTgvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=fefd3369b8164d3cf3d67b205c638f6dd13f21ad35395f53351a583225ec4fb9]
against the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) for
records from the National Institutes of Health (NIH) related to a
federally funded human fetal tissue research program at the University
of Pittsburgh (_Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Health and
Human Services_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36788&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtaGhzLXUtcGl0dC1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDQ0OTgvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=3b38c652533440bf30670786beed178ade21e9c7f4604c8c579f4f1da127c820
_(No.
1:25-cv-04498)).

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the National Institutes of Health, a component of Health and Human
Services, failed to comply with a May 30, 2025, FOIA request for:

> All documents and communications of officials in the Office of
> Extramural Research concerning the Final Research Performance
> Progress Report (FRPPR) for the GUDMAP program at the University of
> Pittsburgh (Project Number U24-DK110791-01).

The National Institutes of Health’s Office of Extramural Research
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36782&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmloLmdvdi9pbnN0aXR1dGVzLW5paC9uaWgtb2ZmaWNlLWRpcmVjdG9yL3Jlc2VhcmNoLWZ1bmRpbmctY29vcmRpbmF0aW9uP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=844e503d90162e991e49568eb07769eba30d7fc2e296636809a43117638dd902]
provides guidance to institutes in research and training programs
conducted through extramural (grant, contract, cooperative agreement)
programs.

The GenitoUrinary (reproductive and urinary) Development Molecular
Anatomy Project (GUDMAP
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36778&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9wdWJtZWQubmNiaS5ubG0ubmloLmdvdi8yMTY1MjY1NS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=835603939de017da8d6b57d9782b6c8c7f4abd8f3d625e527a387affa4340223])
is a research consortium that receives funding from the National
Institutes of Health. The abstract for project number1U24DK110791-01
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36779&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9yZXBvcnRlci5uaWguZ292L3Byb2plY3QtZGV0YWlscy8xVTI0REsxMTA3OTEtMDE/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=5bfab2a0b129f7b2a7fed5d6e7df899fb3e03d18d5e629a2c2a23cff0fd39383]
is titled
“University of Pittsburgh as the GUDMAP Tissue Hub and Collection
Site.” The fiscal year 2016 grant for $600,000 supported the
procurement, processing, and distribution of human fetal tissues. The
abstract states in part:

> The Health Sciences Tissue Bank (HSTB) at the University of
> Pittsburgh has been involved in human tissue procurement for over 18
> years ... HSTB has an established program accruing fetal tissues.
> The fetal tissue IRB has been in place since 2005. HSTB has the
> infrastructure for dissecting specimens and collecting different
> tissue types. In this calendar year, we have disbursed over 300
> fresh samples collected from 77 cases. The collections can be
> significantly ramped up as material could have been accrued from as
> many as 725 cases last year. We have preliminary data showing that
> we can isolate the human urogenital system (kidneys, ureters and
> bladders) from various developmental ages (6-24 weeks). We have
> produced publication quality images of these genitourinary organs
> (including kidneys and bladder) and have also been able to isolate
> and expand cells from various genitourinary organs.

JUSTICE DEPT. SUED FOR PLANNED PARENTHOOD, HUMAN FETAL TISSUE RECORDS

The sale of human fetal tissue is a grisly business at odds with the
values of most Americans, and transparency is the minimum requirement.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36762&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLXBsYW5uZWQtcGFyZW50aG9vZC1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDQ0OTcvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=64d18c0045711731fd5d5d0cc6ba45a479d6f3017503d630ce7aab8890d1cfbe]
against the U.S. Department of Justice for records of communications
between senior officials and key members of the U.S. Senate Judiciary
Committee concerning Planned Parenthood, abortion, and the transfer of
human fetal tissue (_Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of
Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36762&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLXBsYW5uZWQtcGFyZW50aG9vZC1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDQ0OTcvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=64d18c0045711731fd5d5d0cc6ba45a479d6f3017503d630ce7aab8890d1cfbe]
(No.1:25-cv-04497)).

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after
the Justice Department failed to respond to May 30, 2025, FOIA
requests to both the Office of Legislative Affairs and the Office of
the Attorney General for all communications from January 1, 2019, to
December 31, 2020:

> *
> Between officials in the Office of Legislative Affairs and Sen.
> Chuck Grassley, Sen. Lindsey Graham, and/or the Senate Judiciary
> Committee concerning Planned Parenthood, abortion, and/or the
> transfer of human fetal tissue.
>
> *
> Between then-Attorney General Bill Barr and Sen. Chuck Grassley,
> Sen. Lindsey Graham, and/or any official on the Senate Judiciary
> Committee concerning Planned Parenthood, abortion, and/or the
> transfer of human fetal tissue.

In June 2019, the U.S. Senate Committee on the Judiciary issued a
letter
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36764&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYXJ5LnNlbmF0ZS5nb3YvaW1vL21lZGlhL2RvYy9HcmFoYW0gR3Jhc3NsZXkgTGV0dGVyIHRvIERPSiBGQkkgb24gSHVtYW4gRmV0YWwgVGlzc3VlLnBkZj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=799abaebaccb83be630df3e411f49de15604dd96323457e1ac09fac50f047baa]
to Barr and FBI Director Christopher Wray, following up on
Grassley’s 2016 referral of eight organizations for investigation
and potential prosecution for the alleged sale of human fetal tissue
and inquiring as to whether the FBI had taken any action on the
criminal referrals. The FBI has never confirmed publicly whether it
investigated Planned Parenthood, or if it did, what it found.

During a September 2019 hearing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36765&F=T&&hash=ee3a33914c1312c2a83188e3fd362a92be653346c3ae278879b98d56b0ca0759]
at the San Francisco Superior Court, David Daleiden of the Center for
Medical Progress provided testimony regarding his release in 2015 of
11 undercover videos showing Planned Parenthood officials discussing

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36766&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY2xpbmlzY2llbmNlcy5jb20vZW4vbWVyay9DZ3QtZ2xvYmFsLSUyOHN0ZW1leHByZXNzJTI5P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=090581d584e337c2080683691845ef3d6013e142ae7c9c1b9eebff42ebc5a09e].

In April 2020, invoices confirmed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36767&F=T&&hash=bebdf01017b1ca2a8658839aabd483ce7ac0511b386437ff6b67979926d67c03]
Planned Parenthood charged StemExpress $55 per “usable” organ —
not simply for transport fees, as Planned Parenthood had previously
claimed.

TRUMP ADMINISTRATION BLASTED FOR ENDING $10 MILLION TOILET PROJECT IN
MADAGASCAR

When you close the spigot of money flowing from U.S. taxpayers, people
will complain to the press, as is the case with a toilet project in
Madagascar. Our _Corruption Chronicles_ blog has the story
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36784&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdXNhaWQtMTBtLXRvaWxldC1wcm9qZWN0LW1hZGFnYXNjYXIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=0b6195cc6fd4f17e23c4188140527e5bfd04a7b4c1a1ea6c8910f89aa0c707e4].

> administration eliminated from the U.S. government’s generously

> African nation with toilets after a smaller American-funded effort
> to help villagers in the country build toilets with local materials
> failed. The multi-million-dollar remedy, part of Obama’s mission
> to end extreme poverty, focused on sanitation by installing special
> toilets, known as MVP1, in the east African island of Madagascar.
> Over the weekend a mainstream newspaper published a lengthy hit
> piece
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78714&L=36790&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnBvc3QuY29tL2hlYWx0aC8yMDI1LzEyLzI2L3RvaWxldC1zYW5pdGF0aW9uLWludGVybmF0aW9uYWwtYWlkLXVzYWlkLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=563a674cddfdf856a4189adfd51fdb115bb8fb1ad07a2204cea73ec110b61e29]
> chastising the Trump administration for eliminating the costly
> African toilet endeavor funded by the United States Agency for

clean_body: Suing for Human Fetal Tissue Research Records [INSIDE JW] PRESIDENT TRUMP REAPPOINTS TOM FITTON TO DC JUDICIAL COMMISSION [] I am pleased to announce that President Trump has reappointed me to the District of Columbia Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure [] for a five-year term. He initially appointed me to the commission in 2020. I issued this statement about the reappointment: Thank you, President Trump, for reappointing me to the District of Columbia Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure. I am honored and humbled by your confidence. This important responsibility is of course in addition to my duties as Judicial Watch president. I look forward to my continued work with my fellow commissioners to ‘maintain public confidence in an independent, impartial, fair, and qualified judiciary, and to enforce the high standards of conduct judges must adhere to both on and off the bench.’ Our nation’s capital needs the best possible courts, especially in light of the public safety concerns that have gained national attention. The District of Columbia Commission on Judicial Disabilities and Tenure was established by Congress to review complaints of misconduct against judges of the District of Columbia courts. The commission’s authority includes reappointment evaluations (sent to the president) of associate judges and performance and fitness reviews of senior judges. The commission [] has “the authority to remove a judge for willful misconduct in office, for willful and persistent failure to perform judicial duties, and for conduct prejudicial to the administration of justice, or which brings the judicial office into disrepute. The Commission also has the authority to retire a judge involuntarily if the Commission determines that the judge suffers from a mental or physical disability which is or is likely to become permanent and which prevents, or seriously interferes with, the proper performance of duties. In addition, the Commission may, under appropriate circumstances, censure or reprimand a judge publicly.” JUDICIAL WATCH SUES HHS FOR HUMAN FETAL TISSUE RESEARCH RECORDS Americans have a right to know basic information about the taxpayer-funded abortion industrial complex. As long as the government continues to fund these gruesome projects, we will work to expose them. We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) for records from the National Institutes of Health (NIH) related to a federally funded human fetal tissue research program at the University of Pittsburgh (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services [ (No. 1:25-cv-04498)). We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after the National Institutes of Health, a component of Health and Human Services, failed to comply with a May 30, 2025, FOIA request for: All documents and communications of officials in the Office of Extramural Research concerning the Final Research Performance Progress Report (FRPPR) for the GUDMAP program at the University of Pittsburgh (Project Number U24-DK110791-01). The National Institutes of Health’s Office of Extramural Research [] provides guidance to institutes in research and training programs conducted through extramural (grant, contract, cooperative agreement) programs. The GenitoUrinary (reproductive and urinary) Development Molecular Anatomy Project (GUDMAP []) is a research consortium that receives funding from the National Institutes of Health. The abstract for project number1U24DK110791-01 [] is titled “University of Pittsburgh as the GUDMAP Tissue Hub and Collection Site.” The fiscal year 2016 grant for $600,000 supported the procurement, processing, and distribution of human fetal tissues. The abstract states in part: The Health Sciences Tissue Bank (HSTB) at the University of Pittsburgh has been involved in human tissue procurement for over 18 years ... HSTB has an established program accruing fetal tissues. The fetal tissue IRB has been in place since 2005. HSTB has the infrastructure for dissecting specimens and collecting different tissue types. In this calendar year, we have disbursed over 300 fresh samples collected from 77 cases. The collections can be significantly ramped up as material could have been accrued from as many as 725 cases last year. We have preliminary data showing that we can isolate the human urogenital system (kidneys, ureters and bladders) from various developmental ages (6-24 weeks). We have produced publication quality images of these genitourinary organs (including kidneys and bladder) and have also been able to isolate and expand cells from various genitourinary organs. In August 2021, we and the Center for Medical Progress uncovered [] Health and Human Services records that revealed nearly $3 million in federal funds were spent on the University of Pittsburgh’s quest to become a “tissue hub” for human fetal tissue ranging from six to 42 weeks gestation. In July 2023, we filed two amicus curiae [] (friend of the court) briefs to the Supreme Court of the United States in support of The Center for Medical Progress [] (CMP) and its founder Daleiden, asking for review of the Ninth Circuit Court’s affirmation of a monetary award against CMP (Center for Medical Progress et al. v Planned Parent Hood et al [ [] (22-1168)), the other asks for review of an injunction granted to the National Abortion Federation that prevents CMP from publishing more abortion-related videos (Center for Medical Progress et al. v National Abortion Federation [] (22-1135)). In April 2022, we obtained records [] revealing that the Associate Senior Vice Chancellor for Science Strategy and Planning in the Health Sciences at the University of Pittsburgh, Dr. Jeremy Berg, contacted then-Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH), Francis Collins requesting help to combat, “efforts to undermine important science using fetal tissue.” Additionally, the records included a scientific report containing information about grafting human scalp and other tissues onto mice. In September 2021, we uncovered records [] from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) involving “humanized mice [ research with human fetal heads, organs and tissue, including communications and contracts with human fetal tissue provider Advanced Bioscience Resources (ABR). Most of the records are communications and related attachments between Perrin Larton [], a procurement manager for ABR, and research veterinary medical officer Dr. Kristina Howard [] of the Food and Drug Administration. In April 2021, we obtained records [] from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) showing the agency spent tens of thousands of taxpayer dollars to buy human fetal tissue from California-based Advanced Bioscience Resources (ABR). The tissue was used in creating “humanized mice” to test “biologic drug products.” In March 2021, we sued [] the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) on behalf of the Center for Medical Progress for grant applications related to the use of human fetal tissue (Center for Medical Progress v. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services [] (No. 1:21-cv-00642)). In June 2020, we uncovered records [] showing the FDA between 2012 and 2018 entered into eight contracts worth $96,370 with Advanced Bioscience Resources (ABR) to acquire “fresh and never frozen” tissue from 1st and 2nd trimester aborted fetuses for use in creating “humanized mice” for ongoing research. In February 2020, we received records [] from the National Institutes of Health (NIH) showing that the agency paid thousands of dollars to a California-based firm to purchase organs from aborted human fetuses to create “humanized mice” for HIV research. JUSTICE DEPT. SUED FOR PLANNED PARENTHOOD, HUMAN FETAL TISSUE RECORDS The sale of human fetal tissue is a grisly business at odds with the values of most Americans, and transparency is the minimum requirement. We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against the U.S. Department of Justice for records of communications between senior officials and key members of the U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee concerning Planned Parenthood, abortion, and the transfer of human fetal tissue (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No.1:25-cv-04497)). We sued in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after the Justice Department failed to respond to May 30, 2025, FOIA requests to both the Office of Legislative Affairs and the Office of the Attorney General for all communications from January 1, 2019, to December 31, 2020: Between officials in the Office of Legislative Affairs and Sen. Chuck Grassley, Sen. Lindsey Graham, and/or the Senate Judiciary Committee concerning Planned Parenthood, abortion, and/or the transfer of human fetal tissue. Between then-Attorney General Bill Barr and Sen. Chuck Grassley, Sen. Lindsey Graham, and/or any official on the Senate Judiciary Committee concerning Planned Parenthood, abortion, and/or the transfer of human fetal tissue. In June 2019, the U.S. Senate Committee on the Judiciary issued a letter [] to Barr and FBI Director Christopher Wray, following up on Grassley’s 2016 referral of eight organizations for investigation and potential prosecution for the alleged sale of human fetal tissue and inquiring as to whether the FBI had taken any action on the criminal referrals. The FBI has never confirmed publicly whether it investigated Planned Parenthood, or if it did, what it found. During a September 2019 hearing [] at the San Francisco Superior Court, David Daleiden of the Center for Medical Progress provided testimony regarding his release in 2015 of 11 undercover videos showing Planned Parenthood officials discussing — and in some instances displaying — the harvesting and sale of aborted fetal organs and tissue. A non-physician who performed surgical abortions for Planned Parenthood testified that she regularly provided fetal tissue from abortions and was aware of financial exchanges with StemExpress []. In April 2020, invoices confirmed [] Planned Parenthood charged StemExpress $55 per “usable” organ — not simply for transport fees, as Planned Parenthood had previously claimed. TRUMP ADMINISTRATION BLASTED FOR ENDING $10 MILLION TOILET PROJECT IN MADAGASCAR When you close the spigot of money flowing from U.S. taxpayers, people will complain to the press, as is the case with a toilet project in Madagascar. Our Corruption Chronicles_ blog has the story []. Among the many wasteful foreign aid programs the Trump administration eliminated from the U.S. government’s generously funded global health strategy is a $10 million venture to provide an African nation with toilets after a smaller American-funded effort to help villagers in the country build toilets with local materials failed. The multi-million-dollar remedy, part of Obama’s mission to end extreme poverty, focused on sanitation by installing special toilets, known as MVP1, in the east African island of Madagascar. Over the weekend a mainstream newspaper published a lengthy hit piece [] chastising the Trump administration for eliminating the costly African toilet endeavor funded by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID), the bloated State Department offshoot that annually disbursed tens of billions of dollars with little oversight or accountability before the president dismantled it. Now, “only the rich can afford a toilet,” in Madagascar the recently published newspaper story declares as if that should be a concern for American taxpayers. It marks the latest of extensive mainstream media coverage criticizing Trump’s razing of USAID—and questionable programs like the African toilets it bankrolled—while conveniently failing to report the pervasive fraud, waste and corruption that long gripped the foreign aid agency. Judicial Watch has exposed the crisis for years, reporting on a multitude of reckless USAID projects, including Kamala Harris’ failed multi-billion-dollar effort [] to curb “irregular migration” from three targeted countries known as the northern triangle—El Salvador, Guatemala, and Honduras—and millions of dollars to promote leftwing billionaire George Soros’ radical globalist agenda in Latin America as well as a fruitless Clinton-backed port and power plant in Haiti. Judicial Watch has also taken legal action to uncover details about some of the agency’s problematic secret operations. In 2024 we sued [] [ for records involving $27 million in Gaza grants that went to “Miscellaneous Foreign Awardees.” The Biden administration claimed the recipients could not be disclosed because the agency’s workers could be put at risk by Israel. Earlier this year Judicial Watch filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against USAID for failing to provide records about fraud and abuse tied to American aid money sent to Ukraine. The multi-million-dollar African toilet project is just a shred of the broader problem, but worth noting because it provides the famously leftist establishment media with material to continue trashing the administration for redirecting its priorities involving global health strategies. Rather than dubious costly projects like toilets, the new focus is on preventing infectious disease outbreaks from reaching American shores, saving lives and preventing babies from being born with HIV/AIDS rather than sanitation in third-world countries. “Our global health programs have become inefficient and wasteful,” the State Department’s latest America First Global Health Strategy [] reveals. The 40-page document outlines a comprehensive vision that requires co-investment from recipient governments and a path to decreased dependency on assistance from Uncle Sam though other donor nations, such as Germany, Britain, Sweden and France, have followed the U.S. lead in drastically reducing foreign aid. The recent newspaper article criticizes the Trump administration for failing to mention sanitation from its new global health strategy because it excludes projects like the African toilet venture. “Madagascar’s government, recently toppled in a military coupe, has a punishingly long list of problems to deal with and spends just a tiny fraction of its budget on sanitation,” the news story states, adding that “studies have shown widespread contamination of Madagascar’s drinking water, the result of feces buried in pits seeping into the ground.” The article does acknowledge that USAID paid $100,000 to clean up waste in Madagascar before cutting the MVP1 program, though part of the money was used to offer residents a free upgrade to an MVP2 toilet. The U.S. will continue helping impoverished African nations, the State Department assures, but the focus of the multi-billion-dollar funding will be on combatting malaria and maternal-child health as well as global health security. The administration will give Madagascar more than $134 million in assistance under a bilateral global health agreement [] announced last week. The deal also includes over a billion dollars for Ethiopia, $106 million for Botswana and $30 million for Sierra Leone. clean_body: Friends, As we kick off the new year, I am asking you to help us start 2026 with strong fundraising numbers. Keep me fighting for you. | Friends, As we kick off the new year, I am asking you to help us start 2026 with strong fundraising numbers. | | In Washington, talk is cheap. Results are not. I didn’t make excuses. I delivered. Promises made. Promises kept: ✓ Secured the border and backed deportation of criminal illegal immigrants ✓ Stopped New York City’s non-citizen voting law in court ✓ Delivered the largest tax cut in American history ✓ Protected Social Security so seniors keep more of what they earned | That is what results look like. And this is only the beginning. But we can only continue this work if Republicans keep the House majority. And that means winning in November. I am the only Republican member of Congress from New York City. Democrats are coming hard for this seat because they know how much is at stake. | | | | | | | | | | | | | The work continues only if we win. I wish you and your families a happy, healthy and prosperous new year. Thank you for standing with me...now on to victory in 2026! Sincerely, Nicole | Nicole for New York, PO Box 60487, Staten Island, NY 10306

# Friday Focus
---
| | | **January 2, 2026**
---
| [DONATE](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=88cbe35470&e=dd5af5b7b1)
---
| | Welcome to the Friday Focus: your end-of-week snapshot from Team Mai with updates, upcoming events, and ways to plug into our people powered campaign.
---
|
---
| |
# [News & Updates]()
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |
### **Happy New Year from all of us at #TeamMai**

_**2026 is going to be OUR year.**_ We're building something incredible together—a campaign where everyone has a seat at the table. Whether you're fired up and ready to go or just dipping your toes in, we've got a spot for you.
To start off the new year, we’re offering you 3 ways to join #TeamMai _right now_ :
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=27d2949b4a&e=dd5af5b7b1)
| | |
## **Street Team (Field Academy Launching!)**
Big news! We're launching our Field Academy and we want YOU in it.Never knocked a door? Never organized before? Perfect—we'll train you on everything. This is how we build the biggest, most powerful grassroots movement our region has ever seen. Block by block, conversation by conversation, we win.
---
| [Join the Field Academy](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=29efa80a3c&e=dd5af5b7b1)
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=bd07137df8&e=dd5af5b7b1)
| | |
## **Comms Hive**

Are you a storyteller? Expert tiktoker? Put those scrolling skills to work! Help #TeamMai create content, share our message, and make sure everyone knows what we're building. From your fingers to the timeline—you've got this.
---
| [Join the Comms Hive](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=14d73706b3&e=dd5af5b7b1)
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=da7990c8a0&e=dd5af5b7b1)
| | |
## **Events Team**

Know how to throw a good time? We need you. Host a house party, invite your people, and help us grow this movement one living room at a time. Snacks, stories, and solidarity—that's how we do it.
---
| [Join the Events Team](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=290e472547&e=dd5af5b7b1)
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |
Whether you've got 5 minutes a week or 5 hours a day, #TeamMai needs YOU.This is a people-powered campaign, which means it only works if we all show up. Head to maiforus.com/hub and find where you fit. We're saving you a seat.
**Let's make 2026 unforgettable,**
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=0154f86bb9&e=dd5af5b7b1)
| | |

### **Campaign T-Shirt Now Available!**

A **$50 donation** gets you our exclusive campaign shirt, and **every dollar goes straight to powering our people-funded campaign** — amplifying our story, boosting our field operations, and reaching voters in every corner of the district.
Let’s wear the message and build the movement.
[**Get yours here: www.maiforus.com/tshirts**](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=b9acb2d87a&e=dd5af5b7b1)

name: Dave McCormick
subject: How an early morning church service strengthened my faith
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick
body:
This is a deeply personal story

| | | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z7vr0mg40p3p1hr8cc6som9gf5ju19aigonl2n3o_rp22sh2s8i6eor7c5gm6p34cdh2bs)
---
| | I want to take a moment today to share a personal story with you, Friend
It’s a story about how an early morning church service and a sermon referencing Psalm 121 left an imprint on my soul and strengthened my faith.
When I arrived at West Point as a new cadet, I had no idea how much it would transform my life.
During my first plebe summer, I attended an early morning church service. The sermon referenced Psalm 121:
**“I lift my eyes to the mountains- where does my help come from? My help comes from the Lord, the maker of heaven and earth.” Psalm 121**
**That psalm and my time at West Point have been a touchstone ever since, sustaining me during difficult moments in my life.**
When I decided to run for U.S. Senate, I made a promise to come to Washington and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up business as usual. Now I’m working to fulfill that promise every single day.
We need leaders in DC with the strength, the will, and the moral clarity to meet the challenges we face. **So, if you’re looking to continue the status quo, I’m not your guy.**
But if you want to help me continue my fight to change the way things are done in DC, I need your support. Our average online donation is $21.38, and **_[I would be so grateful if you would generously give that amount today.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zppme7cv9lqkrq3fd863tb7rv3oagm31nljkipmo_rp22sh2s8i6eor7c5gm6p34cdh2bs)_**
I’m no stranger to fighting for what is right, and ****if we don’t continue pushing every single day to clean up the mess the career politicians made, we will lose America as we know it!****
There are too many pressing issues we must solve to hold back now. Whether it’s illegal immigration and the fentanyl crisis or inflation and the cost of living, we cannot afford to lose ground.
Here’s where I need your help, Friend I hate asking for money — I’m not a career politician, and it’s not in my nature — but I have no choice but to ask if we’re going to continue fighting to advance our cause to change how things are done.
Democrats from all over the country are panicking and pulling out all the stops to attack Republicans who are shaking up Washington. That’s why I’m hoping you can spare **_[$25, $50, or even $100 to help me fight back.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z8j9m7m4orhkvrtt089k4s01h81r92unnn65be4o_rp22sh2s8i6eor7c5gm6p34cdh2bs)_**
---
[DONATE](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zo265m969250nc7o67mob3hvn5ft8qjmlls2ubt0_rp22sh2s8i6eor7c5gm6p34cdh2bs)
| | Please, join me today to fix our broken government and save what makes America worth fighting for.
Thank you,Senator Dave McCormickArmy Veteran, USMA'87
---
[DONATE](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zauldbmbgppc2549ck4nssg0qevrqjpj4ugfahp0_rp22sh2s8i6eor7c5gm6p34cdh2bs)
| | |
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z75l5find9f1mssheqd8pdar5ktsd64dmjtsoqto_rp22sh2s8i6eor7c5gm6p34cdh2bs)
---
| | I'm Dave McCormick. I'm a West Point grad, combat veteran who served in the 82nd Airborne, a 7th-generation Pennsylvanian, **and last November I defeated an entrenched career politician in the #1 swing state of the 2024 election.**
I’ve seen firsthand what great leadership looks like. I’ve seen that even on our worst days, you have to put the needs of others first.
That’s why I made a promise to come to the Senate and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up Washington, and I’m working to do that every single day.
Whether it’s securing the border, reducing crime in our cities, ending the fentanyl epidemic, or bringing down the cost of living–**I’m committed to delivering concrete solutions to the biggest issues we face.**
But in order to get the job done, it’s going to take all hands on deck. I need every freedom-loving American to help me shake up Washington. I promise I won't let you down.
_Senator Dave McCormick_
---

Our average online donation is $21.38, and I’m no stranger to fighting for what is right, and **if we don’t continue pushing every single day to clean up the mess the career politicians made, we will lose America as we know it! There are too many pressing issues we must solve to hold back now. Whether it’s illegal immigration and the fentanyl crisis or inflation and the cost of living, we cannot afford to lose ground. Here’s where I need your help, Friend I hate asking for money — I’m not a career politician, and it’s not in my nature — but I have no choice but to ask if we’re going to continue fighting to advance our cause to change how things are done. Democrats from all over the country are panicking and pulling out all the stops to attack Republicans who are shaking up Washington. That’s why I’m hoping you can spare | | Please, join me today to fix our broken government and save what makes America worth fighting for. Thank you,Senator Dave McCormickArmy Veteran, USMA'87 | | | | | | | I'm Dave McCormick. I'm a West Point grad, combat veteran who served in the 82nd Airborne, a 7th-generation Pennsylvanian, and last November I defeated an entrenched career politician in the #1 swing state of the 2024 election. I’ve seen firsthand what great leadership looks like. I’ve seen that even on our worst days, you have to put the needs of others first. That’s why I made a promise to come to the Senate and work with fellow political outsiders like President Trump to shake up Washington, and I’m working to do that every single day. Whether it’s securing the border, reducing crime in our cities, ending the fentanyl epidemic, or bringing down the cost of living–I’m committed to delivering concrete solutions to the biggest issues we face.** But in order to get the job done, it’s going to take all hands on deck. I need every freedom-loving American to help me shake up Washington. I promise I won't let you down. Senator Dave McCormick | | | | | Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick | | | Use of candidate’s military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Army or the Department of Defense. ### District 46,
I hope you all had a joyful and restful holiday season.

As we look towards the new year, I hope you are feeling energized and hopeful for all we can achieve together.

The 2026 Legislative Session will be back in full swing in just two days, and I am excited to share some of the things I have been working on in preparation for the critical weeks ahead.

Whether it is fighting to ensure Hoosiers all throughout our state are protected from rising utility bills, ensuring equitable health care in Indiana or advocating for public safety through safe gun storage, I want an Indiana that works for all of us.

I am so proud, every single day, to stand shoulder to shoulder with you all in advocating for a future Indiana that honors both the generations before us and those that will come after us.

Thank you so much to every one of you who takes the time to stay informed, stay engaged and encourages others to do the same.

Our government works best when you are a part of it.

As we approach the remainder of session, I challenge you to make that extra call, send the extra email or testify at a committee that is hearing a bill that will impact you and your family--**this is your government. **
**Don't forget to use[iga.in.gov](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=e74cfdd9d089a93ba9464eecc04cdfaa1541da9d6c066329f67cf1f94c51a86d56d974b390d418f3f203b9ac10055b88407e9867029ca7dc "iga.in.gov") to follow legislation, watch committees and find more information to stay involved. **
---

### Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program
The Energy Assistance Program's (EAP) application is now live until April 20, 2026. You can apply [online](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=e74cfdd9d089a93beb94a184cc7ce5518c5361b15dd303ba60a96e7b582bc85320d2d6e8dbbbbb8fc94ecbc6d18fdd8c33a694d415fbce68 "https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.in.gov/ihcda/homeowners-and-renters/low-income-home-energy-assistance-program-liheap/__;!!FlTQaCg!i0IlNmwztWy24u3OfjflEyvBopYLxqtX54ZBOGpkRIGHfA-g0F4tvn2RkRjwIClxVywwPaZ8QxM8BqUkhHdM0-IFVw$"), by mail or in person. To apply in person or request a paper application form, applicants must contact their local service provider (LSP). For more information on which LSP serves an area, be sure to check the map available on Indiana's [LIHEAP site](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=e74cfdd9d089a93beb94a184cc7ce5518c5361b15dd303ba60a96e7b582bc85320d2d6e8dbbbbb8fc94ecbc6d18fdd8c33a694d415fbce68 "https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.in.gov/ihcda/homeowners-and-renters/low-income-home-energy-assistance-program-liheap/__;!!FlTQaCg!i0IlNmwztWy24u3OfjflEyvBopYLxqtX54ZBOGpkRIGHfA-g0F4tvn2RkRjwIClxVywwPaZ8QxM8BqUkhHdM0-IFVw$").

In addition to this program, Indiana's utilities have financial assistance programs available, as well as other tools that can help customers manage their bills. You can find _[AES's program here](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=e74cfdd9d089a93bb46f0a90e98158c9f64b65c002648147885b8ab028d201152540c2d0e66ae2512ee82496dac737cabf0f790aefb2ac72 "https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.aesindiana.com/payment-assistance__;!!FlTQaCg!jwpfitqHKx4bP-5cnhYvoAX1m8Q0kv3P901-pv7dMHXQdpRgas_bRhhF66AOaSuHe8tMuc2p6moGi_XjArAanOEmX3Q$") [.](https://click.subscription.in.gov/?qs=e74cfdd9d089a93b4f9f59ce35d24021b697c647dd130f987d93b35840535a6d17ee3bc3a54ef1fd7803ac23c4d0ffc7a8b860dd281c513d "https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.nipsco.com/bills-and-payments/financial-support__;!!FlTQaCg!i0IlNmwztWy24u3OfjflEyvBopYLxqtX54ZBOGpkRIGHfA-g0F4tvn2RkRjwIClxVywwPaZ8QxM8BqUkhHfV2_ol6Q$")_

As a reminder, the winter moratorium runs from December 1 through March 15 and prevents disconnection of residential energy utility service to any customer who is eligible for and has applied for the Energy Assistance Program. Moratorium protection applies once a household submits an application and allows the Local Service Provider time to determine eligibility.

Utility bills continue to rise, and what should be an essential service has become an overwhelming burden for many families. If you're eligible, please take advantage of the available resources so you don't have to choose between keeping food on the table and keeping the lights on.

name: Troy Downing
subject: This isn’t a drill. They’re coming after MAGA.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Peter, do you consider yourself a true Patriot? If you do, I’m BEGGING YOU to read this email...

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-bMdUEM2rY7Nn34I_Ca7w)Hey you!
Yes, you, Peter.
No, not your laptop. The person looking at it!
I was really hoping to reach you today.
This is Congressman Troy Downing, and I want to thank you for opening my message. Can I borrow just one more minute of your time?
**President Donald J. Trump and House Republicans have accomplished a historic twelve-month run unlike anything in modern American history.**
_🇺🇸 We secured the border 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We brought back American jobs 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We cut taxes for working Americans 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We reduced government waste 🇺🇸_ _
__🇺🇸 We eliminated the woke agenda 🇺🇸_ The tide has turned in our favor.
**_BUT!!! BUT!!! BUT!!!_**
**WE CAN’T REST ON OUR LAURELS NOW!!!**
The Democrats are more desperate than ever.
They’ve doubled down on extremism with their unhinged socialist economic policies, woke DEI agenda, and aggressive climate change plan.
They’ve become a dangerous cabal that **CANNOT BE TRUSTED** with America’s future.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. Iranian officials warned Friday that U.S. troops and bases across the region [would be “legitimate targets”](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO4yAQxvGnwZ0jPGB8LiiucZdrr7SwGcKsMFgwSZS3X3lfYetP-ku_7_99vTtKlB__3IGWQ1kzvltCZqwrVZfJZY4VHbcNmT9bKWfDFSSYXg69VKt3lD6r7rwNiICqQztMZtLqj9JDFy16CWGb932bUCs_G4UGnNrQz6Mcgu_IXjU5SDUMctTy5sOozebAaAU-7EpoiZ641NtXocwRQ_qUF9bbXo4u2ch8NqH-ClgELBwxUkrXJmCJ5cALJGBx_qBMjatjKlnAMhozG1B9ohf2z9M7xtZzfR5nf8EFLF21_nxTStTMJLR8HI5-yh3_6qqXhe8AAAD__5sNfss) if Washington intervenes in Iran’s ongoing economic crisis, responding to remarks by President Trump.

# Saturday, January 3, 2026
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1777, [the Battle of Princeton](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM9u2zAMxp9GvtnQP8vJQQdvQ24ZhmHAjoZsUTELWTJkNmkuffYiToE-QE8kf_g-gvz-n4ezw4jp8tstYCnkIcFti0AEZcDiErpEcwFH2whE9zHndYNBcmlqLmquBu8w3gddeRsAJKgKrOhMp9VBaVHNlgdjxFEB9zyM3vvjUalJGgiHtgUAU6F9bOOCKyF4q3njQ6vN6KTRSvowKaY5eKRcmpeMiWYI8Z6vUJopL1W0M9G6MdUzeWLytI6b6LquyeXC5IlmqEdHFKHOoV4Lpgkop12qmOrfmeoJ3oipX_9mYLKV_Mcu39sc9vLny_aAicmf6-OQfcQnpU93v0DByT3hX7jm-EqYU1MV69cbxoib6Zjml8Vh3B-gb6V-tfIjAAD___8RkrU) delivered a crucial victory for George Washington and the Continental Army, proving American forces could defeat the British and shifting momentum toward independence.

If you love pasta, you're going to love this news: Tariffs on certain Italian pastas were set to soar an additional 92%, causing panic among pasta buyers. But now, as you can [hear this morning on the _Flyover Podcast_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUgOHTwBYLeITQgaFLtnTtiJ7NI6YiYOGnpL591V6hB_h_6fu8xRuWWtr9Ax8UOPfY6LVXYqYRy8BWsPE6CHmfifmYe992ikYZd1L6pCAmLPWIVqaQiQyBpKAv7mLBg9VyDfnNk0aXZ4DkM3g4owHvQC1LStp6WcLvTWkFWquzVVPKZ-tmNM6CSXkBYRWlwn1MX700XinXoz9pTEt_yBpW5m0X8C7MVZjroHlgS1M9hLluPdE3DzRyhLS9Sq1ldxdh1f2Bpf71_C_0M5ifAAAA__93emtx), the U.S. has agreed to drop the extra fees and keep the macaroni flowing.

Today's sponsor, [Dr. Steven Gundry MD](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMGKszAUhZ8m7ipJ7m2iiyx-KHbV8jMMdCnRXGtmopZ4W-nbD84rzPJwDueD73ZpLz6mON-vfiLHw9LOtK2JmCm3Mfs5-pnHTJ7Xjpjf3bI8Vmq11OYg1UFCG3xM7xaL4AYiTVCQU9ZYhApQFaOzHrDu0Vc91kdCUMbqqlK1HUyn69oW0e1vUklQSh5RlmE4oum8Ngg6DD0IlBQiL7n8WuLMIw3pvbwol_0yFcmNzI9VwD-hG6GbbdvKkKfM-XvvhW4-zvZ6PQndwMV-nv_f9h00zxgEnCyg0GZ9dmoPCqDILjy2mFJcjRUo75OP6RfEf7LzcvonAAD___NXdIY), is calling out a popular breakfast many of us grew up with and revealing why he says it may be quietly undermining your health--and what to eat instead.

## US, Iran Trade Threats
Iranian officials warned Friday that U.S. troops and bases across the region [would be “legitimate targets”](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO4yAQxvGnwZ0jPGB8LiiucZdrr7SwGcKsMFgwSZS3X3lfYetP-ku_7_99vTtKlB__3IGWQ1kzvltCZqwrVZfJZY4VHbcNmT9bKWfDFSSYXg69VKt3lD6r7rwNiICqQztMZtLqj9JDFy16CWGb932bUCs_G4UGnNrQz6Mcgu_IXjU5SDUMctTy5sOozebAaAU-7EpoiZ641NtXocwRQ_qUF9bbXo4u2ch8NqH-ClgELBwxUkrXJmCJ5cALJGBx_qBMjatjKlnAMhozG1B9ohf2z9M7xtZzfR5nf8EFLF21_nxTStTMJLR8HI5-yh3_6qqXhe8AAAD__5sNfss) if Washington intervenes in Iran’s ongoing economic crisis, in response to posts by President Trump. On Truth Social, Trump said his administration was “locked and loaded and ready to go” to rescue “peaceful protesters” if Iran shoots or “violently kills” them. Protests in Iran began nearly a week ago with shopkeepers angry over the collapse of the rial, the nation’s currency, and grew into street fights between protestors and government forces. [Death toll estimates](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L9ugzAQBvCnMZuR_wFh8NCFLV07Igcf4SpjI_sSmrev3DxC19OnT_f7vq7z1WHAeP90O1ha0xzhLAGIIM-YXUQXacvgqNyA6HVL6SgwK6F6LiQXevYOw2s2jbcrgALdgJVDPxh90UY2mx0uo_GL78zgYdHuojro9SjXYfBKOjM2aGubkEJLKTojWr92pr851Rut_LpoZgR4pJTb74SRNljDKz0ht0vam2A3oqMw_cHUxNR0nmd7urJhvFOK8ON2jO8oU1O11UzKwTM1GT32Qo9MTVXK306I_FE45QrllB_7wY-cdixQOEaC_IRImGK9EhSqhU22_jgxBCz9wIy47w7D33v0r02fVv0GAAD__-_9jk0) ranged from five to eight by Friday. Iran’s top security official, Ali Larijani, accused the U.S. and Israel of exploiting the unrest for new attacks, urging Americans to heed the safety of U.S. troops.
## Famous Classics Enter the Public Domain
Thousands of cultural works from 1930 [entered the public domain ](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DtuhDAQgOHTmM7IL-xs4SIN3aZNifwYlomMjYx3EbePWOUG6X5ppNF8832f7g4T5seXW8G2uUwZjj1Ba1AnrC6jy22p4NruobXTl7LtMAkmNGWcMjlFh-mcVBftDCBAdmC50UbJD6l4t1ijHVP-BhqYiZEPgXHg8WbETQ2a-dihvbYxziTnbFCsj_OgtHdCKyniHCRRDCK2UvufgrktMKezvKD2oaxdsktr207kJxEjEeNxHH324VJccyLGK4kYnzv9qzeEXhLqU8kRgWaXw0ljhYNCblAxP-j29AkDjWV1mOlbXEN2YuBGy67auB2YEu7aEMUeq8P0Pqj964svK34DAAD__5b6h3U)on Jan. 1, including Betty Boop, Nancy Drew, Disney’s first Pluto, and novels by William Faulkner, Dashiell Hammett, and Agatha Christie. Other works entering the public domain span early cinema, featuring the early Marx Brothers, Bing Crosby’s film debut, and best picture winners _All Quiet on the Western Front_ and _Cimarron_. Selections from [the Great American Songbook](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2unTAQhJ8Guj3yDxhOQZGG7qZNiRZ7fXBkbGQvF_H2EfcV0o9GM9_352v5whBD-vzGnSb2eUl01UjMVJZQMAVMvBVCrisx32vOR6VFCWVASBB6cRjivXStmzyRIt3SJAczdHrUnWy3aZDvUXqNxpr34KTqtZFOiV6bfhxXWtswPW1CCi2l6Dvxcr7vzIrKdFo5b3XTCXKBc3n9zSHxRj7e-ZvKy-a9jdPGfNRG_2rU3Kj5uq5X3QNvNT_bd_w8sUbNWLiCPSOfhRo1b3gcNxznGoMFl3cMCRzewBkwRri2DJYirQWZ4M4nWEyQ8gVnJfhBAQ8LSJjsDa7QBZgc8EawY2SqBB6jzQl8LuALEchRvMfhrYZGzW2Z3HGFGEM1Q9OJz44h_hzi_7LwPal_AQAA___fZp7n), _Georgia on My Mind_ and _Dream a Little Dream of Me_ , also entered the public domain, opening the classics to unrestricted use. The 2026 public domain additions represent works that reached the 95-year U.S. copyright limit, allowing creators to use and repurpose them freely without permission or payment.
## What's Ahead in Space for 2026
This year will mark humanity's return to lunar space with [four astronauts launching](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2O2zAQhJ-G6mjwz7pzocJBICDFpbki6YS1uLQ2oEiBu7Ggtw90r5BqigFm8M38-pg-gDKV509YcZBUp4I7ZxTBNlGDQlBkaQjCDxQ5HrVujJMzrtfGauOnCJSPKXRxSIgOfYeDfevfgn_3wXbLcHs3N-NCsLcrxpRcb2-QZudMQpv6q-9oONOMNd5acw3mEtM19A9wffAuptmrYDCS1Hb5U6nIgikf9YXtMte1y8MisrHyd-VG5cZ93y8rl9NTbsSi_7JyI8-EZUblRmBptdT1UG4swKDxQNYbvapA1l9cO8mioQmuxJpIr7UWvRIz1aIL7lowI891Qw0l6owQsfFCm54XKE88C6Hp-91-_v5BUfmxzqd8_3bujDt_Ytu6NsRtp5yJ-zcVzHMFyl9E8l83vAb3LwAA___K7p7e) on a 10-day flyby mission early this year that will loop behind the moon's far side for the first time in more than 50 years. Meanwhile, commercial companies including [Blue Origin and Firefly Aerospace](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO3CAQhJ8Gd1iw7OH8BUUad5c2pYXN-rwRBgvwOff2kZNHSDcajT7NzM_n9PQcOb1--J1cW_OU6KqRWqMycfGJfWpbId_qTK195pyPShMosFJpqcwUPMfPhF1wKxGQ6cjpwQ5ovhnU3eYsrGAQUVsz-Dl8oQqIyxKsBYsQsGN305RWRmv1QNWH9YF29mDRQFgXI1BR4JZL_ytzahut8ZPfVPol7110W2tHFea7gFHAeF1XT2fJ94w7IGBM9LsJGEHBQ8Co4dZf_wwrOcl6-IXknnOSO9fKOVVZqJ0lSVoiH5WqrBfRIW_wQdKnIP1ZcvFVLjk1Tid1xYXj4hi52kGgeu2e49-G7b9ufTv4EwAA__888YyX) are racing to land uncrewed spacecraft on the lunar surface, with China's Chang'e 7 probe targeting the moon's south pole to hunt for frozen water. Closer to home, [astronauts will conduct spacewalks](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1ygzAQBOCngU6M_gymUJGGzmlTMgc6zDmyxEhnGN4-4-QRUn7Nzu5-3cYbUKB4_4QnOl7SGPEoAZkxj5QhEkReMwKXCZnPKaWt4KilboVUQprRA4VztLV3C6JGU6NTXdtZczVW1auble47DVIuiPPSo5x033svWyXntr_2Nbl3mlTSKCUvVjZ-udh2At1ao_0ym8pK9MQpN49EkVdcwpl2zM2cnnVwK_NWKvNR6aHSw3EcTYQCzT3tlR7ea0TGgFDwTSggOIkt4054iFcRZYMZDwjfRQD_SRQGphQFRfGA-IJ8Vnqos_PbQSFQabvKyvsTKPx24H8dtzv9EwAA__-aqoSC) outside the International Space Station in January to install solar arrays and upgrade equipment.

For 2025, space news included an array of spectacular photos from sophisticated camera and powerful telescopes. Here are 10 of the [best space photos of the year.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uhDAQRk9jOiN7bGC3cJGGbtOmRAOMwZHByJ4sIqePdq-Q-vvR0_t6DA8MMezLJ27k2Kdhp7NEYqY8hIx7wJ3XTMhlJOZrTOkoNICCViotlRlmDPEabDU7TwRkKnK6aztrbsbqanXjBNjY6T5qbVtFAB3etB9Bd8rcR6AquNeb0sporRqr6tk3th0RWmtg9pMRVtEcOOX6O4WdV_LxSk_K9ZS2KrqV-SjCfAjoBfTnedblwIleqYC-MOYFf8O-COixcE7HmjgtGY_1EtCnnyy1kh6fKQcm-d7KKW0yE86U5bteZPISFDRVdvNxhhhDaTth1bJhiG8Q_pe9p4O_AAAA__-4RoVw)

It might be doing more harm than good...

For years, big food companies told us cereal was a healthy way to start the day.

But now, one top doctor is sounding the alarm...

“It turns out, most cereals are packed with hidden sugars that can cause weight gain, low energy, and irregular bowel movements,” he says.

In this short video, Dr. Steven Gundry reveals what’s really in your morning bowl of cereal — and what to eat instead.

clean_body: Iranian officials warned Friday that U.S. troops and bases across the region if Washington intervenes in Iran’s ongoing economic crisis, responding to remarks by President Trump. | | | | | | Saturday, January 3, 2026 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1777, delivered a crucial victory for George Washington and the Continental Army, proving American forces could defeat the British and shifting momentum toward independence. If you love pasta, you're going to love this news: Tariffs on certain Italian pastas were set to soar an additional 92%, causing panic among pasta buyers. But now, as you can , the U.S. has agreed to drop the extra fees and keep the macaroni flowing. Today's sponsor, , is calling out a popular breakfast many of us grew up with and revealing why he says it may be quietly undermining your health--and what to eat instead. | US, Iran Trade Threats Iranian officials warned Friday that U.S. troops and bases across the region if Washington intervenes in Iran’s ongoing economic crisis, in response to posts by President Trump. On Truth Social, Trump said his administration was “locked and loaded and ready to go” to rescue “peaceful protesters” if Iran shoots or “violently kills” them. Protests in Iran began nearly a week ago with shopkeepers angry over the collapse of the rial, the nation’s currency, and grew into street fights between protestors and government forces. ranged from five to eight by Friday. Iran’s top security official, Ali Larijani, accused the U.S. and Israel of exploiting the unrest for new attacks, urging Americans to heed the safety of U.S. troops. Famous Classics Enter the Public Domain Thousands of cultural works from 1930 Jan. 1, including Betty Boop, Nancy Drew, Disney’s first Pluto, and novels by William Faulkner, Dashiell Hammett, and Agatha Christie. Other works entering the public domain span early cinema, featuring the early Marx Brothers, Bing Crosby’s film debut, and best picture winners All Quiet on the Western Front and Cimarron. Selections from , Georgia on My Mind and Dream a Little Dream of Me , also entered the public domain, opening the classics to unrestricted use. The 2026 public domain additions represent works that reached the 95-year U.S. copyright limit, allowing creators to use and repurpose them freely without permission or payment. What's Ahead in Space for 2026 This year will mark humanity's return to lunar space with on a 10-day flyby mission early this year that will loop behind the moon's far side for the first time in more than 50 years. Meanwhile, commercial companies including are racing to land uncrewed spacecraft on the lunar surface, with China's Chang'e 7 probe targeting the moon's south pole to hunt for frozen water. Closer to home, outside the International Space Station in January to install solar arrays and upgrade equipment. For 2025, space news included an array of spectacular photos from sophisticated camera and powerful telescopes. # Saturday, January 3, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1874, Claude Marie Dubuis purchased property in Galveston for [St. Mary’s Orphanage](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2SozAQQOHTiAxKP1iCQMEmZN50Q0pYDXRtI3mltjG335q5wlzgvarvz32-ByRM2-9wgOfPmucEZyVghjKvlHMkDAsS8rWWF_JK179XmLXUtpWqlWaOAema-yb6RcZRNeCVs67vlXVDs3s5DHpU9madMUYZGIzqlyW6RxztqKNr0H_FpJJGaamU7Aa5gB2CdTA-bqu0opcQkXPpeAeGT6grXfkNpXvkoyG_Mz-rML-EnoSezvPsuO4hJ8IEXS6b0NMeUlxy_iv0BIkLQhV6qtweoVy1zeW5hxQ2aLdAb6icU1N8fJ5IhNU60cvtCEjfP_6Z0tvr_wEAAP__qB19kQ), which remained in operation until 1968. Dubuis was the second Catholic bishop of Texas and the founder of Sisters of Charity of the Incarnate Word in the U.S. A Killeen woman received an incredible gift from her husband this Christmas in the form of [his own kidney.](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOnDAQBOCnMZ3R2uYMFC7S0F3alMjgNazOwAUvx_H2EXmFv5lmpE-j-fPsn54SrdNvv6Djb9z6Fc-ckBn3PqZtC4n8QIn4ivtBHNP19_C9Bm0lKAmmD57S1VdFcAOEVhXoVG3rqlK2borZQfUwBnXbGI_YQqNGC02wUfn4MKaFgtyNgQKjNCgFZQMD2sbbGtvxEcGKCjAQb3vJMzJ-fY7p2j64l-O2FMnNzO8szC-hO6G78zzL18nfuxS6u22hO1B3aKG7-ciDX4Oc6INZnhRRHolp8YxynHfKvPgsJ4osZ8ryRWHFS-iu2F14n5QSZVuLCqbFU_q_gH_228fpfwEAAP__S8aBZw) After five years of dialysis, Shatera McDowell got news that her husband, Damarkius, was a perfect match for a kidney donation. Today’s sponsor, [Chill Country](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkE1ugzAQhU9jdon8Q2xYeNEqYpduu7QMHoepDE7NkITbV1TpCbqd75unp_d5cRePCefrh5_A0jNmN8NjSUAExcWUc0joe0xIWywrUkzb9-qd5FIfuDhw5YLHtLm6CrbnoRUVWGG0qWuhTVONNirRt60WIBtlTDSm7WXPT7HlbS9ORlRo9zAuuBKSC8GPDe9BN14baIdT5JrVHAJSLkcageDpl5i2fIdyHPJUJTsS3Ram3pjsmOyGEVMa8jpT2XaBye6lM9WtNLklr2UAps40ApPvf1DqHU4QcJ2YOsPkMb2Og59uHq8zU-dQNvfl59WXzUldFRtuD0wJF21Yza_7028r-t-Wdyt_AgAA__9HNYrU), is a Texas-born brand crafting clean, high-quality edibles designed to help you relax, unwind, and feel your best—with a special Dry January discount just for Flyover readers.

## Court: Feds Liable for Harvey Damage
A federal appeals court has ruled that the federal government is [liable for flood damage](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu3CAQRb8Gd1gwZrFfQZHG3Uub0sIw2CgYNoDX67-PWOUP0o00M-fq3F_fy7f2wcftpz5Q1bdLS8SrBKwV8-JCSjZ4vfrg6-3y6asL959TL8BAUsYpGxarfbgX0Vm1MvvFO1R8lKMQXI5Ttyu0Rkqx2nWSnA_TYwIxrMJZI1ejjZWdVw3GOBs4MM5ZP7EV5aTliF_m4ZgkgqH1NeW-7ljxrYsLd3ph7k06uqD2Wp-FDD8IzATm67p6E7z5DXs6S02xXRGYmxaB-fNPYAYGDwIzhzYLAvPWiPHAWGkzDkhdytTqQ29Ia6J7OrDQiDrTf-C2LNSe2ceN7mfO3uiIdNf5hTf9dFeoSWeuNJ8BW2qXlX1ePgRf5EgE2w7tw0ej_l_5LwV_AwAA___ALpxt) to Houston-area homes during Hurricane Harvey, citing mismanagement of the reservoirs formed by the Addicks and Barker dams. The three-judge panel ruled unanimously in favor of homeowners, finding that the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers “was aware or should have been aware” that the dams, built as protection for downtown Houston areas downstream, could cause flooding in the areas upstream. In 2017, Harvey dumped enough rain to fill the Addicks and Barker reservoirs to unprecedented levels, [flooding neighborhoods](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGynDAQRE8DmbZGg5AgIHBC9p06pAY0-qg8u8JCLMvtXfgKjl91V_f79TV9UZT4-v5JTx7KJ6TpxecuXArnKUhKXiLNUWK5Qj5iCXL9OWhCQKtAK2gmT1GuydR-mMH3uuZBO-uM0dZ19Towt2FhQy1Tw33LXejn4LXG0PWtA6jjcJeBhkYjaA2PDma2HVnH_dIGsJUB9rGk_CgrF_7QHuRKb86PJT1rGdZStr1qflQ4Vjie5_n4vabjhhWOlEtchCsc718VjpIWkhtsG5PsaklHLmo9co4LvVitlN98qXzcVtSanpzOF-c7il2reucdYutVY5peGWBS_YyLckjAFu3MBus8-O2MInG3rjLw_aQo_8aW_1P8HvBvAAAA__-9DJAK) inside federally owned flood-control reservoirs that contained 14,000 homes at the time of the hurricane, a third of which suffered flood damage.
## Fruit Flies Prompt 1,000-Acre Quarantine
More than 1,000 acres of farmland in Hidalgo and Cameron counties have been [quarantined](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOgzAQRU9jOtDYGAOFi23osu2WyMA4jGRsAkMIt18le4Ttn57--z-3_uYoULx_uwUtv3zqI557QGbceh9SmgK5gQLx5beD2IfrcbhegTI5yBzKfnIUrl5nkx1gamWGVtam1lqauslm60vpJSD4spE11E2pq6EcXdX6VoNumozsWwYSSqlASigaGNA0ztTYjpUHIzTgRJy2gmdkfLndhys9cSvGtGTBzszrLsovoTqhuvM8i-XaXXSRU6T0hoTq3lVCdXs6eM4_EqE6tzGNAYXqFnzR6GL-acx9uP6Y_HG4zUWmiHuupDIGZFWs85ptdlpPCoF2UwsN98VR-Ozh_734tOo3AAD__9fgiBM) since early December after the discovery of invasive fruit flies from Mexico, known as Mexflies. Mexflies are especially harmful to citrus as a preferred place to lay eggs. The larvae then burrow into the fruit, rendering it unmarketable. People in quarantined areas are prohibited from [transporting produce](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGupSAURb8GO8wBFbwFxTR2b9opDcrhepKjOMjT599PvL8w9U5W9lp_vsYvT0zb-7df0ZWfmMYNr4OxFMxj5JQCk5-Iqdwxf1OJfP_99qMGbSQoCc0YPPE9tlVwE4SXqtApa2zbKmP7anHRQP9SHhprVehRt2qauk534BsEi01F7oGBgkZpUArqHiY0vTcWX3MXwYgWMFBJuS4LFvzxR-Q7nZjrOa0Vu6WU_RDNL6EHoYfruup5yWl7VqGHR0fowedCM6PQA22nP-hE-fGRkW8Z6JgfIAY5-xVz2uRCwfM7Sa206XVj6n3Zq-zCfhEzHcaKFt6rJ_6cKP-X7nT6XwAAAP__4g-D0g) to places outside of the quarantine and are told to dispose of all wasted fruits and vegetables, double-bagged, via their municipal trash service. Mexflies were temporarily eradicated from Texas in 2012 before making a return in 2024.

The three-judge panel ruled unanimously in favor of homeowners, finding that the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers “was aware or should have been aware” that the dams, built as protection for downtown Houston areas downstream, could cause flooding in the areas upstream. In 2017, Harvey dumped enough rain to fill the Addicks and Barker reservoirs to unprecedented levels, inside federally owned flood-control reservoirs that contained 14,000 homes at the time of the hurricane, a third of which suffered flood damage. Fruit Flies Prompt 1,000-Acre Quarantine More than 1,000 acres of farmland in Hidalgo and Cameron counties have been since early December after the discovery of invasive fruit flies from Mexico, known as Mexflies. Mexflies are especially harmful to citrus as a preferred place to lay eggs. The larvae then burrow into the fruit, rendering it unmarketable. People in quarantined areas are prohibited from to places outside of the quarantine and are told to dispose of all wasted fruits and vegetables, double-bagged, via their municipal trash service. name: The Maine Wire
subject: Silence Is Not Leadership — And the Walls Are Closing In on Janet Mills
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Silence Is Not Leadership — And the Walls Are Closing In on Janet Mills (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=dd1bc2a414&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Jan 02, 2026 05:44 pm
The walls are closing in on the Gateway Communities scandal, and Governor Janet Mills remains silent. What started as questions about MaineCare billing has now grown into a national-level credibility crisis. Congressional scrutiny is underway, with the House Oversight Committee, chaired by Kentucky Representative James Comer, now leading the inquiry into how taxpayer dollars were [...]
